conflicts.description: Death location is described as Queens in early oral accounts, then East New York/Brooklyn in later testimony and certified records.
conflicts.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:1688 ("motel room in Queens"); snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:1716 ("motor lodge in Queens"); snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:2910 ("short-stay motel room in East New York"); snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:4291 ("Location: Harbor Motor Lodge, room 214"); snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:6372 ("motel ... in Queens"); snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:6718 ("Borough of Brooklyn").
conflicts.canonical_value: Tomas died on November 12, 1992 at Harbor Motor Lodge, room 214, in Brooklyn (East New York area).
conflicts.resolution_note: Certified municipal record references and repeated document-based chapters (11/12/18) are more causally embedded than stressed oral recollections in chapter_06 and location drift in later memory retelling.

conflicts.description: Leo is narrated as nine years old in chapter_12 but called eight years old in chapter_14 dialogue.
conflicts.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:4253 ("He was nine, in fourth grade"); snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:5123 ("You're eight years old").
conflicts.canonical_value: Leo is nine years old (fourth grade) during cycle 02.
conflicts.resolution_note: The narrated age plus grade-level placement is internally coherent and better anchored for downstream continuity than a single contradictory dialogue line.
